text:
Once upon a time in the quiet village of Eldergrove, there lived a man named Harold. Harold was infamous among his neighbors for his peculiar habit. Every morning, while the sun was just peeking over the horizon, he'd go out into the woods behind his house.

What set him apart wasn't his morning rituals; many villagers engaged in similar activities. Nor was it his gentle gait or his love for collecting oddities; there were plenty of collectors. What intrigued people was how Harold never took anything out of the woods.

All year long Harold would browse, filling his odd assortment of treasures.

During the harvest festival, Harold's collection was the talk of the town. A bird wing here, a rusty hinge there, a spider silk thread. People marveled at the variety. How could one man have such an impressive collection?

You see, Harold wasn’t collecting for personal use. He was gathering to give away. Every now and then, he'd host little 'finds'
